name: Barranco del Val Trail
name_original: Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – running circular desde Ágreda (Senda Barranco del Val)
description: Hike the moderate 8.2-mile Barranco del Val Trail in Sierra del Moncayo, featuring a waterfall and a viewpoint.
region_intro: Hiking around Ágreda offers a diverse landscape for exploration. The region is characterized by its valleys, such as the Barranco del Val, and features natural attractions like the Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas. Hikers can expect varied terrain, including paths through forests and routes leading to rocky outcrops and hills. Best hiking trails around Ágreda The most popular hiking route is Barranco del Val Trail, an 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda (PR-SO 20 y 20.1 Senda Barranco del Val), a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This circular route features a waterfall and a prominent rock formation. Local hikers also love the Peña Negrilla Loop, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Ágreda is defined by its valleys, waterfalls, and forested areas, offering a range of experiences from moderate valley walks to more difficult climbs to rocky hills. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Ágreda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Ágreda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ágreda?
A: There are over 70 hiking trails around Ágreda, offering a wide range of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging climbs. The region is known for its diverse landscapes, including valleys, forests, and rocky outcrops.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Ágreda offers several easy hiking trails. There are 11 routes specifically graded as easy, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails typically feature gentler terrain and shorter distances.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ágreda?
A: Many of the easier trails around Ágreda are suitable for families. Look for routes with shorter distances and minimal elevation gain. These paths often lead through pleasant valleys or forested areas, providing an enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Ágreda?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails around Ágreda. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in nature areas, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for other hikers. Please check specific trail regulations if available.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region boasts natural attractions like the Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas. You can explore this beautiful waterfall on the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057585100/cascada-del-pozo-de-las-truchas-y-pena-del-tio-fresquito-circular-desde-agreda-pr-so-20-y-20-1-senda-barranco-del-val" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, which also includes a prominent rock formation.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Ágreda?
A: Yes, Ágreda offers several circular routes, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057585100/cascada-del-pozo-de-las-truchas-y-pena-del-tio-fresquito-circular-desde-agreda-pr-so-20-y-20-1-senda-barranco-del-val" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas y Peña del Tío Fresquito – circular desde Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056716529/hayedo-de-la-hoyada-circular-desde-aldehuela-de-agreda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hayedo de La Hoyada – circular desde Aldehuela de Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a beautiful beech forest.

Q: What kind of viewpoints can I expect on the trails around Ágreda?
A: Hiking around Ágreda offers diverse viewpoints, from scenic valley vistas to panoramic mountain outlooks. Notable spots include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, which provide excellent perspectives of the surrounding landscape and the majestic Moncayo.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, Ágreda's surroundings are rich in history and natural beauty. You can visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3743913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Vozmediano&lt;/a&gt; or explore charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama&lt;/a&gt;. These attractions offer a glimpse into the region's heritage and complement your hiking experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Ágreda?
A: The best time to hike in Ágreda is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring and colorful foliage in autumn. Summers can be hot, while winters may bring snow to higher elevations.

Q: Are there options for winter hiking in Ágreda?
A: While some higher-altitude trails might be challenging or inaccessible due to snow in winter, many lower-lying routes in Ágreda remain suitable for hiking. Winter offers a unique, tranquil experience with crisp air and potentially snow-dusted landscapes. Always check local weather conditions and trail reports before heading out.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Ágreda?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes starting directly from Ágreda or nearby villages will have designated parking areas or roadside spots. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as users often provide information about parking facilities near the starting points.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Ágreda?
A: The trails in Ágreda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from scenic valleys to challenging climbs, and the natural beauty, including waterfalls and forested areas. The well-maintained paths and varied difficulty levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Ágreda?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Ágreda offers several difficult routes. There are 13 trails graded as difficult, often featuring significant elevation gain and rugged terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056705826/pena-negrilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negrilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which involves substantial climbing and takes around 5 hours to complete.
tips: Walking along the bottom of the Val ravine, we come across a sign that indicates the detour to the Peña del Tío Fresquito, which we will access by a small staircase and a subsequent climb. If we can do it, it is worth it for the good views it offers us of the Val valley.
Beautiful waterfall located in an environment of peace and nature in the Cañón del Río Val. The area is well signposted with indicator and informative signs.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Barranco del Val Trail?
faqs.answer: The Barranco del Val Trail is located in Montes de Zuera Castejon de Valdejasa, Zaragoza. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you'll typically find access points and parking options near the start of the trail within this area.

faqs.question: Is the Barranco del Val Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, for hiking, the trail is rated T1 on the SAC scale, indicating an easy level of difficulty. The physical rating is moderate, and the path is generally easy to follow, making it suitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features singletrack terrain with narrow vegetation, particularly within the ravine (barranco). You can expect some delicate, slow, and intricate passages. It is primarily a downhill route, offering a unique experience through a landscape shaped by water erosion.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Barranco del Val Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can discover notable natural features. Highlights include the scenic Peña del Tío Fresquito and the beautiful Pozo de las Truchas Waterfall.

faqs.question: Is the Barranco del Val Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is dog-friendly, so you are welcome to bring your canine companion along for the adventure.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its ravine nature and narrow passages, spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike. These seasons offer milder temperatures and typically avoid the intense summer heat or potential winter conditions that could affect the delicate terrain.

faqs.question: Does the Barranco del Val Trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Senda Barranco del Val, Camino Jacobeo Castellano-Aragonés: Tarazona-Ágreda, and Camino Jacobeo Castellano-Aragonés: variantes.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for accessing the Barranco del Val Trail itself.

faqs.question: Does this trail require any special permits or fees as it's located in Sierra del Moncayo?
faqs.answer: While the Barranco del Val Trail is in the broader region, it is specifically located in Montes de Zuera Castejon de Valdejasa, Zaragoza. There are no special permits or fees required for this particular trail.

faqs.question: Is the Barranco del Val Trail suitable for activities other than hiking?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is quite versatile. It's popular for trail running, mountain biking, and e-biking. Class 1 e-bikes are specifically allowed on the route.

faqs.question: What should I know about the trail's direction or flow?
faqs.answer: The Barranco del Val Trail is primarily a downhill route. This means you'll experience a consistent descent for much of the journey, which can be a strategic consideration for planning your hike or ride.
